revenue_lines.notes: Three new enterprise contracts closed in November
Steady growth from Q3 pipeline conversion
Two implementation projects pushed to Q1 2026
Increased consumption from financial services vertical
New certification program launched in October

expense_lines.notes: One senior hire deferred to January
Additional spend on Q4 conference sponsorships and lead gen campaign
Contractor brought in for enterprise onboarding backlog
Compute scaling for new enterprise workloads

burn_rate: N/A — cash flow positive since Q2 2025
executive_summary: Q4 2025 was the strongest quarter in company history by revenue, driven by enterprise SaaS growth and API consumption. Total revenue reached $4.95M, up 9.3% from Q3. Operating margin improved to 20.9% despite slightly elevated sales and marketing spend, which was a deliberate investment in pipeline building for H1 2026. Three new enterprise logos (Nordea, Orica, and Chubb) contributed $420K in new annual contract value. Professional services revenue declined due to project timing, not demand — the backlog entering Q1 is the largest in 12 months.
risks_outlook:
1. Two enterprise renewals totaling $680K ARR are due in Q1 — both accounts are flagged as healthy but require exec-level engagement
2. AWS infrastructure costs are trending 8% above forecast due to higher-than-expected GPU usage for the new ML features — evaluating reserved capacity commitment
3. Sales hiring plan calls for 4 additional AEs in Q1; slower-than-expected recruiting could impact H1 pipeline targets
4. The competitive landscape is intensifying — two well-funded startups entered the mid-market segment in Q4 with aggressive pricing
